sector.name: Pipes & Tubes
sector.description: Pipe and tube makers scaling capacity

fyPerformance: Q4 volumes grew 9% YoY.
Q4 EBITDA/ton was above 5,500.
FY26 ROCE stood at 37%.
Operating cash flow generation was around 2,000 Cr and free cash flow around 1,300 Cr.
Company closed FY26 with net cash balance above 1,500 Cr.
highlights: Long-term 8 MnT capacity plan by FY28 remains on track.
Pending capex for 8 MnT plan is around 1,400-1,500 Cr.
Annual capex target remains around 500-600 Cr.
Dubai operations are currently at around 40% utilization.
Management targets long-term EBITDA/ton of 5,000-5,500.
May-June volume recovery was expected after temporary disruption.
APL Apollo Tubes Ltd.quote: "Our long-term plan of 8-million-ton capacity by FY28 remains totally on-track."

fyPerformance: FY26 consolidated revenue crossed 4,100 Cr.
FY26 consolidated EBITDA grew to 418.5 Cr.
FY26 PAT grew 10.2% to 182.6 Cr.
Q4 EBITDA grew 11.7% YoY to 104.2 Cr.
Q4 PAT including OCI stood at 48.5 Cr.
highlights: Q4 standalone sales stood at 1,061.5 Cr.
FY26 standalone EBITDA grew to 395.8 Cr.
FY26 standalone PAT grew to 173.4 Cr.
Specialized product category supported realizations.
Renewable and solar energy products remain focus areas.
Financial discipline remained an important FY26 theme.
Goodluck India Ltd.quote: "FY26 has been another important year for Goodluck India."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 23% to 1,667 Cr.
FY26 EBITDA grew 19% to 209 Cr with margin at 12.56%.
FY26 PAT grew 23% to 76 Cr.
Q4 revenue stood at 507 Cr with strong growth.
Q4 PAT grew 75% YoY to 30 Cr.
highlights: Q4 EBITDA grew 31% YoY to 64 Cr.
Q4 EBITDA margin stood at 12.59%.
Full-year operating cash flow stood at 192 Cr.
EBITDA to OCF conversion stood at 92%.
ROCE improved to 21%.
FY27 focus is improving capacity utilization.
Hariom Pipe Industries Ltd.quote: "FY26 has been a year of steady performance and improved operating numbers."

fyPerformance: Q4 sales volume grew 27% YoY to 1.47 lakh tons.
FY26 sales volume increased to 5.32 lakh tons.
Q4 revenue grew 102% YoY to 1,480 Cr.
FY26 revenue grew 37% to 4,200 Cr.
FY26 EBITDA grew 8% to 174 Cr with stable EBITDA/ton.
highlights: Q4 EBITDA grew 33% to 46 Cr.
Q4 EBITDA/ton improved to 3,150.
Debt/equity stood at 0.18x.
Additional 1 MnT capacity is planned by FY29.
APA pipes facility readiness is expected by Q4FY27.
Installed solar power capacity reached 16.5 MW.
Hi-Tech Pipes Ltd.quote: "FY26 has been another year of strong operational growth and strategic progress."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue from operations stood at 2,136 Cr.
FY26 EBITDA stood at 166 Cr and PAT at 103 Cr.
FY26 sales volume was the highest ever at 395,900 MT.
Q4 revenue grew 47.5% YoY to 693 Cr.
Q4 EBITDA stood at 58 Cr and PAT at 38 Cr.
highlights: Q4 value-added product share was around 27%.
Mangaon facility utilization improved.
Export sales share reached around 10%.
JTL Defence delivered around 20% EBITDA margin in Q4.
Mangaon capex is nearing completion by H1.
Management expects utilization to move toward 60-70%.
JTL Industries Ltd.quote: "We should be able to deliver about 30% volume growth YoY."

fyPerformance: Q4 standalone sales stood at 893 Cr versus a high base last year.
Consolidated Q4 sales stood at 1,085 Cr.
Order book stood around 2,160 Cr as of 1 May 2026.
Export order book contributed around 700 Cr.
Company remains effectively debt-free on regular bank limits.
highlights: Subsidiary revenue grew 28% in one business during Q4.
Another subsidiary recorded around 60% YoY growth in Q4.
Domestic deemed exports remain a meaningful part of demand.
Energy and infrastructure capex are demand drivers.
Order execution remains lumpy by project cycle.
Margins improved despite sequential sales decline.
Ratnamani Metals & Tubes Ltd.quote: "The order book has continued to be around 2,000 Cr."

fyPerformance: FY26 was a landmark year with volume up 37%, revenue up 60%, EBITDA up 79% and PAT up 147%.
FY26 revenue increased to 2,413 Cr.
FY26 EBITDA stood at 276 Cr with margin above 11%.
PAT more than doubled to 143 Cr with margin improving to 6%.
Value-added product volume grew 51% to 356,000 tons.

guidance.value: Long-term debt below 1.5x net worth/forward EBITDA

highlights: Q4 revenue grew 38% to 685 Cr.
Q4 EBITDA grew 91% to 92 Cr.
Q4 PAT more than doubled to 56 Cr.
EBITDA/ton stood at 9,500 excluding sponge iron sales.
DFT expansion capex is around 50 Cr.
Additional Sarora capex is around 150 Cr.

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 7% to 518.7 Cr.
FY26 EBITDA stood at 76.2 Cr with margin at 14.7%.
FY26 PAT grew 22% to 38.8 Cr, with PAT margin up 100 bps.
Q4 revenue was broadly flat at 123.6 Cr.
Exports contributed 34.6% of FY26 revenue and 44.8% of Q4 revenue.
highlights: Seamless capacity increased to 20,000 MTPA.
Welded capacity to reach 21,150 MTPA by H1FY28.
8.79 MW DC solar project is being installed.
Solar project can save around 8.63 Cr annually in electricity cost.
FY26 capex stood at 110 Cr.
Working capital management remains a focus for FY27.
Scoda Tubes Ltd.quote: "FY27, we are expecting a 25% revenue growth with 14% to 15% EBITDA margins."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 22% to 1,166.8 Cr.

highlights: Exports grew 18% in FY26.
Total installed capacity stands at 27,600 MTPA.
Company is 100% backward integrated for 20,400 MTPA capacity.
Revenue mix in Q4 was 59% seamless.
Seamless revenue grew 18% and welded revenue grew 15% in Q4.
EBITDA to CFO conversion stood at 59% for FY26.
